methods.description: Procedure for preprocessing raw δ18O and dust time series from the NGRIP ice core, including resampling, interpolation, gap filling, detrending, and filtering, to prepare them for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: The dust record was resampled here to the lower 5 cm resolution for consistency with the δ 18 O record.
steps.searchable_summary: resample dust time series to 5 cm resolution
inputs: Raw dust time series (1 mm resolution)
outputs: Resampled dust time series (5 cm resolution)

steps.description: first, both time series are interpolated to an equidistant time axis with 5 a intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate δ18O and dust time series to 5 year intervals
inputs: Resampled dust time series (5 cm resolution)
outputs: Interpolated dust time series (5 a intervals)
Interpolated δ18O time series (5 a intervals)

steps.description: Second, gaps of varying length present in the dust time series and totaling about 6 % of the data points are filled by next-neighbor interpolation.
steps.searchable_summary: fill gaps in interpolated dust time series using next-neighbor interpolation

steps.description: Third, the multimillennial trend is removed from both time series using a 40 ka running mean.
steps.searchable_summary: remove trend from interpolated δ18O and gap-filled dust time series using 40 ka running mean

steps.description: Fourth, the noise level of the δ 18 O record is reduced by applying a Butterworth low-pass filter of order 4, with a cutoff frequency of 0.02, corresponding to a period of 50 a.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Butterworth low-pass filter to detrended δ18O time series
